anchored_threads.thread_name: Sāṅkhya / first-principles knowledge (verses 2.11-2.30). What survives change; what is configuration vs. essence; the imperishable that is the substrate of action.
anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: The verse closes the materialist branch of the sāṅkhya argument: even on the alternative-doctrine reading, the conclusion (do not grieve) holds. The engineering layer respects this by treating the cycle as the operating-shape of the field rather than as a metaphysical claim.

progression_issue.explanation: The verse follows 2.26 (concessive) and precedes 2.28 (three-phase structure); the engineering layer's lineage-argument respects this position by closing the materialist conditional rather than opening a new argument.
